Code fragment: public final TableField<ChatRecord, Long> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is generically named 'id' instead of a more descriptive name like 'chat_id'. Consider renaming the column to 'chat_id' to make it self-documenting and avoid ambiguity when joining tables.

Code fragment: public final TableField<LinkRecord, Long> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table uses a generic 'id' column as the primary key while having a unique constraint on 'url' (LINK_URL_KEY) which could serve as a natural primary key. Consider using the URL as the primary key directly, or rename the 'id' column to something more descriptive like 'link_id'.

Code fragment: public final TableField<LinkRecord, OffsetDateTime> LAST_CHECKED_AT = createField(DSL.name("last_checked_at"), SQLDataType.TIMESTAMPWITHTIMEZONE(6).defaultValue(DSL.field("(now() AT TIME ZONE 'utc'::text)", SQLDataType.TIMESTAMPWITHTIMEZONE)), this, "");
Explanation: The column 'last_checked_at' has a default value but is marked as NULLABLE (no .nullable(false) specified), which is inconsistent since a column with a default value typically should not allow NULL. Consider adding .nullable(false) to the column definition to enforce that it always has a value.

Using `CHAT.fields()` selects all columns from the CHAT table, which fetches unnecessary data and couples the code to the table schema. Instead, explicitly select only the columns needed for the ChatDto, such as `dslContext.select(CHAT.ID).from(CHAT)`.

Using LINK.fields() selects all columns from the LINK table, which can fetch unnecessary data and cause maintenance issues if the table schema changes. Instead, explicitly select only the columns needed for the LinkDto mapping.

Using LINK.fields() retrieves all columns from the LINK table, which is inefficient and couples the code to the full table schema. Specify only the required columns explicitly, such as LINK.ID, LINK.URL, LINK.LAST_CHECKED_AT, etc.

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the LINK table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and hurt performance. Explicitly list only the required columns, e.g., select(LINK.URL, LINK.UPDATED_AT).

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the CHAT table, which retrieves potentially unnecessary data. Explicitly specify only the columns needed for ChatDto, e.g., select(CHAT.ID, CHAT.NAME).
